Click Generate
Strength: -
Password Length Longer is more secure
Uppercase (A-Z) ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ
Lowercase (a-z) abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
Numbers (0-9) 0123456789
Symbols !@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;:,.<>?
Exclude ambiguous chars Excludes l, 1, I, O, 0 (easier to read)

Ad space

Tips for Strong Passwords

  • Use at least 12 characters (16+ recommended)
  • Include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
  • Never reuse passwords across websites
  • Use a password manager
  • Enable two-factor authentication when possible

Is this tool safe?

Yes! All passwords are generated directly in your browser using a cryptographically secure random number generator. Nothing is sent to any server — your passwords stay only on your device.

Made with ❤️ for your security

Home · Privacy Policy

What is Password Generator?

A password generator helps you create unique credentials for work accounts, personal logins, and shared team tools without reusing weak passwords.

Password Generator helps when you need Generate secure passwords in one tab instead of switching to desktop software or a temporary script.

How to use this tool

  1. Choose password length and the character groups you want to include.
  2. Generate a new password until the mix matches your security policy.
  3. Copy the result and save it in a password manager instead of a plain text note.

Common use cases

  • Creating strong passwords for new SaaS accounts and admin users.
  • Rotating credentials after a security review or team handoff.
  • Generating unique one-off passwords for client or vendor access.

Tips for better results

  • Use 16 or more characters for important accounts whenever allowed.
  • Avoid reusing one password across email, banking, and work systems.
  • Pair strong passwords with a password manager and MFA.

Example workflow

length: 20\noptions: upper, lower, numbers, symbols\nresult: uP7!xL92#qT8vN4@dK1s

FAQ

Does this password generator run locally?

Yes. Password creation happens in the browser so you can generate values without a separate desktop tool.

What makes a strong password?

Length matters most. A longer password with mixed character types is usually more resistant to guessing and reuse risk.

Should I memorize generated passwords?

For most accounts, storing them in a reputable password manager is a better workflow than trying to memorize many long random strings.
